title: Behavioral Experiment Log
description: Track your social exposure experiments — planned activities that test your anxious predictions against reality. Each entry builds evidence about the accuracy of your fears.
instructions: Before a social exposure, write your prediction. Afterward, document what actually happened and what you learned.

fields.label: Situation — What did you do? Where? With whom?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Made small talk with a coworker during a coffee break

fields.label: Prediction — What did you think would happen?
fields.placeholder: e.g., 'I'll run out of things to say and they'll think I'm weird'

fields.label: How likely did the feared outcome feel? (0 = not at all, 100 = certain)
Safety behaviors I dropped for this experiment
fields.placeholder: e.g., Didn't rehearse what to say, didn't avoid eye contact, didn't check my phone

fields.label: What actually happened?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Conversation lasted 3 minutes. They smiled and asked follow-up questions. One pause, but they filled it.

fields.label: What did I learn?
fields.placeholder: e.g., Conversations can flow naturally. The other person contributes too. One pause doesn't ruin everything.

fields.label: How likely does the feared outcome seem now? (0 = not at all, 100 = certain)
